items.title: FOBO Is The New FOMO — And It's Supercharging The AI Capex Cycle
items.description: Forget fear of missing out. JPMorgan says the dominant force behind the next wave of AI spending is FOBO — Fear of Becoming Obsolete. And it's pushing both governments and corporations to spend faster, bigger, and earlier than expected. Unlike prior tech cycles driven by optimism, this one is driven by survival. Enterprises and policymakers increasingly see AI as critical infrastructure, not discretionary investment — setting the stage for another year of capex upside surprises. From Hype To Sur

items.description: The S&P 500 sector ETFs delivered strong gains in 2025, led by XLK, XLC, and XLI. Click here to read more about the sectors.

items.title: What The Latest Analyst Revisions Mean For New Oriental Education's Evolving Investment Story
items.description: New Oriental Education & Technology Group’s latest research update leaves its fair value estimate steady at about $64.49 per share, but reflects a slightly higher discount rate as analysts recalibrate risk and return expectations. The modest tweaks in long term revenue growth assumptions stem from stronger than expected top line momentum and improving earnings visibility, even as cautious voices flag potential headwinds in overseas markets and regulation. Read on to see how these shifting...
